summary: A genuine dark horse. Under Sebastián Beccacece, Ecuador built South America's meanest defence — conceding just five goals in 18 qualifiers and finishing second behind Argentina, with 1-0 wins over Chile, Argentina, Colombia and Peru. 2025 was their first unbeaten calendar year since 1994. Marginal football, mastered: 'score one more than the opposition'.
style: A 4-4-2 (switched from a 3-4-3 last March) — disciplined, hard-running, quick to regain shape and counter-press, hitting opponents on the break from middle-third regains. Patient, at times defensive in possession: short goal kicks, neat wide combinations, and direct balls over the top. Rotations drop a pivot between the centre-backs and push the full-backs on (Hincapié high and narrow at left-back, switching to the winger via Caicedo).

counterReliance.note: Elite low block + counter-press, breaking at pace off regains

strengths: South America's best defence — only 5 conceded in 18 qualifiers, eight 0-0 draws
Disciplined running, quick shape-recovery and effective counter-pressing
A golden-generation spine (Caicedo, Hincapié, Pacho) with technical quality and physicality
Veteran reliability — Valencia's goals and Galíndez in goal
weaknesses: Can be defensive with the ball — low on goals (the flip side of 'marginal football')
Discipline lapses — eight red cards across two years (half theirs)

keyPlayers.name: Moisés Caicedo
keyPlayers.note: The engine — wins it back and feeds the wide switches; core of the Independiente del Valle generation (all 24: Caicedo, Hincapié, Pacho)

keyPlayers.name: Enner Valencia
keyPlayers.note: Still leads the line and scores most of the goals — 6 in 6 World Cup appearances (two vs Qatar in 2022)

keyPlayers.name: Hernán Galíndez
keyPlayers.note: Pillar of the record defence — saved 30 of 34 shots faced in qualifying

tactics: Sit in a compact, disciplined 4-4-2, counter-press hard, and win games by the odd goal — patient with the ball, lethal on the regain. The elite defence and golden-generation spine make them a true dark horse, even if goals can be scarce.
notes: Knocked out of Copa América 2024 by Argentina on penalties; responded with an unbeaten 2025.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group E (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
